name: White Hand Gang
description: The White Hand Gang was a collection of Irish American gangs on the New York City, Brooklyn, and Red Hook waterfronts from the early 1900s to 1925. They organized against the growing influence of Italian gangsters, choosing their name as a response to the Sicilian Black Hand gangs. Known for being virulently anti-Italian and particularly violent, the gang suffered from unstable leadership and internal killings, leading to its demise.
